summary: I checked the technical design (`design.md`) against the business requirements (`requirements.md`, R1–R50) for two-way coverage: is every requirement addressed, and is anything designed that no requirement asks for. I independently verified each requirement against the design body rather than trusting its §11 traceability table — all 50 are genuinely satisfied across access/landing, layout/summary, active pipelines, recent activity, fleet health, live updates, responsiveness/accessibility, and the open-questions modal flow. I also confirmed no scope creep: the two notable structural additions (the `open_questions_structured` sibling artifact and removal of the inline define-panel answers form) both trace to R39/R40 and the confirmed Q14 clarifying-question default, and I validated those citations against `open_questions.md`. Verdict: **pass** with empty findings; I noted one non-blocking observation in the summary (the `fleet_health` poll endpoint lacks the `safely` wrapper, so an R33 section-error message wouldn't render on a failed periodic reload — an implementation detail the design's stated intent already covers).
